articles.title: ISU Receives $50M Grant for Renewable Energy Research
articles.summary: Iowa State University has been awarded a landmark $50 million federal grant to advance biofuel and wind energy research over the next five years.

articles.title: Cyclones Clinch Big 12 Regular Season Title
articles.summary: Iowa State men's basketball secured the Big 12 regular season championship after a dominant win in Hilton Coliseum on Saturday.

articles.title: New Student Wellness Center Opens Fall 2026
articles.summary: Construction on the $80 million Student Wellness Center is on track for a fall 2026 opening, featuring expanded fitness, counseling, and health services.

articles.title: CyRide Adding Three New Electric Buses
articles.summary: CyRide will introduce three new electric buses to its fleet this spring as part of the Ames sustainability initiative.

articles.title: Computer Science Department Ranks Top 30 Nationally
articles.summary: Iowa State's CS program climbed to #28 in the latest U.S. News rankings, boosted by AI and cybersecurity research.

articles.title: Spring Break Dining Hours Announced
articles.summary: Most campus dining locations will operate on reduced hours during spring break (March 23–31). Seasons and UDCC will remain open.

articles.title: Veishea 2026 Planning Kicks Off
articles.summary: Student Government announced a community festival for spring, featuring concerts, food, and carnival rides on central campus.

articles.title: Engineering Students Win National Robotics Competition
articles.summary: A team of ISU mechanical and electrical engineering students took first place at the National Robotics Challenge in Detroit.
